==================================================
LAYER 1 — PATTERN + VARIATION ENGINE (NO AI)
==================================================

Goal:
Generate candidate ideas cheaply from reusable viral structures before calling any model.

This layer should run first.

Core principle:
Reuse strong idea shapes, but vary:
- topic
- scenario
- hook wording
- caption
- emotional spike

Do NOT repeat the exact same phrase structure too often.

---

1. Pattern templates

Create a small library of reusable templates.

Examples:

A. Denial loop
- "the way I said I’d [ACTION] and then [REALITY]"
- "why do I say I’ll [ACTION] like I don’t know myself"
- "I really said I’d [ACTION] and then [REALITY]"

B. Expectation vs reality
- "what I say vs what I do when I [GOAL]"
- "me pretending I’m [IDEAL] vs me actually [REALITY]"

C. Small panic
- "why did I just remember [PROBLEM] now"
- "this is where I realized [PROBLEM]"

D. Avoidance
- "the way I act like [TASK] doesn’t exist"
- "I really just ignored [TASK] like it would disappear"

E. Social awkwardness
- "the way I answered [SOCIAL_MOMENT] like a normal person"
- "why did I respond like that"

F. Routine contradiction
- "me saying I’m done with [HABIT] vs me doing it again"
- "I said no more [HABIT] and immediately [REALITY]"

---

2. Topic / scenario pools

Create safe topic pools.

Examples:
- sleep
- cooking
- coffee
- food delivery
- gym
- laundry
- texting
- emails
- work
- errands
- cleaning
- fridge
- outfit
- weekend plans
- social calls
- productivity
- procrastination

Avoid sensitive/private topics:
- bank apps
- balances
- real DMs/screenshots
- medical data
- salary
- IDs
- addresses
- passwords

---

3. Hook style rotation

For each generated candidate, vary hook style.

Supported hook styles:
- the_way_i
- why_do_i
- i_really_just
- contrast
- curiosity
- internal_thought

Important:
Do NOT make every variation start with “the way I…”.

Example same structure, different hooks:

Base:
“I said I’d sleep early but it’s 3am”

Variations:
- "the way I said I’d sleep early and it’s 3am"
- "why do I say I’ll sleep early like I don’t know myself"
- "I really just said I’d sleep early and it’s 3am"
- "me saying I’ll sleep early vs me at 3am"
- "this is where my sleep early plan failed"

---

4. Generate no-AI candidates

For each request:
- Generate 12–20 candidates locally
- Bias toward user memory:
  - top structures
  - top hook styles
  - top emotions
  - top formats
- Include 20–30% adjacent exploration
- Avoid repeating last 2 accepted scenario families if possible
- Penalize repetition, do not hard-ban it

---

5. Output candidate shape

Each candidate must include:

{
  source: "pattern_variation",
  hook,
  whatToFilm,
  howToFilm,
  caption,
  whyThisWorks,
  patternTags: {
    structure,
    hookStyle,
    emotionalSpike,
    format
  },
  scenarioFamily
}

---

==================================================
LAYER 2 — DETERMINISTIC SCORE / FILTER
==================================================

Goal:
Never show weak ideas, whether they came from variation engine or Llama.

Run this on all candidates.

---

1. Hard reject filters

Reject if:

- hook is neutral/descriptive
- hook lacks tension
- idea is abstract
- idea is advice/motivational
- idea says “talk about” or “share your thoughts”
- no visible action
- unclear payoff
- requires private/sensitive content
- requires setup, props, location change, or other people
- caption is generic or repeats the hook

Weak hook starts:
- "POV: you..."
- "when you..."
- "watching..."
- "reading..."
- "talk about..."
- "share your thoughts..."

Exception:
Only allow if rewritten into something personal and tense.

---

2. Score each candidate 0–10

Hook impact: 0–2
- 0 plain/descriptive
- 1 decent
- 2 scroll-stopping

Tension: 0–2
- 0 no conflict
- 1 mild conflict
- 2 clear contradiction / regret / denial / panic / embarrassment / irony

Filmability: 0–2
- 0 too much effort
- 1 doable
- 2 one location, can film immediately

Personal fit: 0–2
- 0 generic / not user style
- 1 somewhat fits
- 2 feels like this creator would say it

Caption strength: 0–1
- 0 generic/repeats hook
- 1 extends moment with thought, contradiction, or specific detail

Freshness: 0–1
- 0 stale
- 1 familiar but fresh angle

Total = 10

---

3. Pass thresholds

- 8–10: keep
- 6–7: rewrite hook/caption once if possible, then rescore
- <6: reject

Hard rule:
If hook score = 0 → reject.
If tension score = 0 → reject.
If filmability score = 0 → reject.

---

4. Rewrite weak-but-promising candidates

For 6–7 candidates:
- rewrite hook
- rewrite caption
- keep the idea structure

Hook rewrite must:
- sound like a real thought/text
- include tension
- avoid neutral phrasing
- be short
- feel personal

Caption rewrite must:
- add internal thought OR contradiction OR specific detail
- not repeat hook
- max 1 emoji

If rescore <8 → reject.

---

==================================================
LAYER 3 — LLAMA 3.1 IDEATOR FALLBACK
==================================================

Goal:
Use Llama 3.1 only when local pattern variations cannot produce enough good ideas.

Llama 3.1 is the low-cost candidate generator, not the final judge.

---

1. When to call Llama

Only call Llama if:
- fewer than 3 candidates pass filtering
- diversity is too low
- user explicitly requests fresh ideas
- memory changed significantly and local patterns are stale

Default:
Try no-AI variation first.

---

2. Llama generation

Ask Llama 3.1 to generate 7–10 candidates.

Each candidate must include:
{
  source: "llama_3_1",
  hook,
  whatToFilm,
  howToFilm,
  caption,
  whyThisWorks,
  patternTags: {
    structure,
    hookStyle,
    emotionalSpike,
    format
  },
  scenarioFamily
}

---

3. Llama prompt requirements

Llama candidates must follow same rules:
- specific moment
- visible reaction
- clear payoff
- low effort
- no private data
- no advice
- no abstract ideas
- no generic POV
- caption extends moment
- max 1 emoji

Generate more candidates than needed because many will be filtered.

---

4. Filter Llama outputs

Run ALL Llama candidates through Layer 2.

Do NOT trust Llama scoring.

Do NOT show Llama output directly.

Only show candidates that pass deterministic filtering.

---

==================================================
LAYER 4 — MERGE + RANK
==================================================

Goal:
Combine local pattern candidates and Llama candidates, then return best 3.

---

1. Candidate priority

Rank by:
1. score
2. personal fit
3. hook impact
4. diversity
5. source preference

Prefer pattern_variation if score is tied, because it costs nothing.

---

2. Batch rules

Return 3 ideas.

Rules:
- At least 2 should align with user memory if possible
- 1 can be adjacent exploration
- Avoid duplicate scenarioFamily
- Avoid duplicate hookStyle if possible
- Avoid all 3 having same structure
- Quality beats diversity

If fewer than 3 excellent ideas exist:
- return fewer with explanation in UI if existing UX supports it
- do NOT show weak filler

---

3. Cache

Cache returned batch per user/day.

If user requests again same day:
- serve cached batch unless user explicitly regenerates
- regeneration should be rate-limited/tracked

---

==================================================
LAYER 5 — LLAMA 3.2 VISION VIDEO UNDERSTANDING
==================================================

Goal:
Use Llama 3.2 Vision as a video understanding/style extraction helper for uploaded videos.

Do NOT use Llama 3.2 Vision as the main ideator.

Principle:
Llama 3.2 Vision = eyes.
Llama 3.1 / pattern engine = ideator.

---

1. When to use

Run on uploaded training videos or uploaded clips when available.

Use it to extract lightweight visual/style hints.

---

2. Output shape

Return structured JSON:

{
  contentType: "talking_head" | "reaction" | "pov" | "mini_story" | "lifestyle" | "unknown",
  setting: "bedroom" | "kitchen" | "car" | "office" | "outside" | "unknown",
  energyLevel: "low" | "medium" | "high",
  visibleAction: string,
  reactionVisible: boolean,
  privacyRisk: boolean,
  privacyRiskReason?: string,
  suggestedFormat: "mini_story" | "reaction" | "pov" | "contrast",
  visualNotes: string[]
}

---

3. What it should detect

- setting
- content type
- whether creator is visible
- whether reaction is visible
- whether clip feels low/medium/high energy
- obvious privacy risks
- whether it supports reaction / mini-story / POV format

---

4. Privacy safety

If Llama 3.2 Vision sees:
- bank app
- visible messages
- IDs
- addresses
- license plates
- medical docs
- salary/paystub
- passwords
- private documents

Set:
privacyRisk = true

Do not generate ideas requiring this content.

---

5. Style profile integration

Use extracted hints to update derivedStyleHints.

Examples:
- frequent low-energy bedroom clips → energyLevel low, setting bedroom
- frequent face reactions → reactionVisible true, suggestedFormat reaction
- talking-head videos → contentType talking_head
- casual home scenes → low-effort home setting preference

---

6. Ideator integration

Pass style hints into generation summary:

Style hints:
- common settings
- visual energy
- preferred visual format
- privacy risks
- visible reaction tendency

Use these to shape:
- whatToFilm
- howToFilm
- caption tone
- hook style lightly

Do NOT let vision hints override:
- quality rules
- memory
- safety
- user feedback

---

==================================================
COST CONTROL RULES
==================================================

1. Pattern + variation first
2. Llama 3.1 only if needed
3. Llama 3.2 Vision only when analyzing uploaded video
4. Cache daily batches
5. Rate-limit regeneration
6. Never run old swarm for Phase 1

Runtime path must NOT call:
- director agent
- editor agent
- monetizer agent
- swarm orchestration
- trend riff loops
- multi-pass revisions

---

==================================================
QA CHECKLIST
==================================================

Test 1: Existing user with strong memory
Expected:
- mostly pattern_variation ideas
- 0–1 Llama calls
- ideas still feel personal

Test 2: New user with no memory
Expected:
- pattern engine produces safe default ideas
- Llama only used if quality/diversity is low

Test 3: User requests regeneration
Expected:
- cached ideas not repeated too aggressively
- Llama call only if local candidates fail

Test 4: Uploaded video analyzed by Llama 3.2 Vision
Expected:
- structured style hints
- no idea generation from vision model
- privacy risks flagged

Test 5: Cost audit
Expected:
- most idea requests use zero external generation calls
- when Llama 3.1 is called, it is one call only
- no swarm calls

---

SUCCESS CRITERION

User-visible quality stays premium.
AI cost drops dramatically.
System remains Phase 1 scoped.
This gives your agent the full layered architecture in one clean pass.

from coding agent : Frozen. Nothing else from me on this — Llama 3.1 stays on the shelf until you've validated the pattern path on real batches.

For your QA list, the orchestrator already logs everything you'll want to track:

Fallback rate — every Claude-fallback request emits a hybrid_ideator.fallback_used log line with localKept, fallbackKept, and creatorId. You can grep server logs or roll it into a counter later.
Pattern repetition across regenerations — the viral_pattern_memory table already records what each creator has seen, and the pattern engine's memory bias plus the regenerate=true salt are what's supposed to prevent same-batch repeats. If you spot dupes within a single creator's session, that's the signal to tune.
Cache hit visibility — source: "cache" in the orchestrator return tells you when a same-day repeat was served for free.
Caption strength — captions come from the same CAPTION_POOLS_BY_STRUCTURE map per structure; if any structure's pool feels weak in real batches, that's a one-line edit in patternIdeator.ts.
If pattern quality holds and you want to wire Llama 3.1 as the middle tier later, the seam is already there — hybridIdeator.ts has the merged.length < 3 branch as the single fallback gate, so it's a clean swap to insert Llama before Claude rather than instead of it.

Send this to your coding agent:

NOVELTY SCORING BOOST — STOP REPETITIVE IDEAS

Problem:
The pattern path is technically working, but the output feels repetitive.
It keeps returning the same safe idea shape:
- “the way I…”
- mini-story
- self-failure loop
- deadpan reaction
- coffee/gym/productivity variants

We need novelty scoring on top of quality scoring.

Goal:
Keep quality high, but prevent batches from feeling like clones.

---

1. ADD NOVELTY SCORE

For every candidate, compute noveltyScore from 0–5.

Dimensions:

A. Hook phrase novelty: 0–1
- 0 if same hookStyle as another selected candidate
- 1 if hookStyle is unused in current batch

B. Scenario novelty: 0–1
- 0 if same scenarioFamily as recent accepted/viewed ideas
- 1 if fresh scenarioFamily

C. Structure novelty: 0–1
- 0 if same structure dominates recent batch/history
- 1 if structure is underused

D. Visual action novelty: 0–1
- 0 if same visual execution appears repeatedly
  e.g. look → react → deadpan
- 1 if it uses a different physical action/scene

E. Topic novelty: 0–1
- 0 if topic repeats recent topic
- 1 if topic is new

---

2. FINAL SCORE

Keep existing quality score 0–10.

Add adjusted score:

adjustedScore = qualityScore + noveltyScore

But only apply novelty boost if:
qualityScore >= 8

Do NOT let novelty rescue weak ideas.

---

3. PENALIZE REPETITION

Add penalties:

- same hookStyle as already selected: -2
- same scenarioFamily as already selected: -3
- same structure as already selected: -1
- same topic as already selected: -2
- same visual action pattern as already selected: -2

---

4. SELECTION RULE

Selection should be:

Step 1:
Filter candidates with qualityScore >= 8

Step 2:
Pick first highest adjustedScore

Step 3:
For each next pick, recalculate repetition penalties against already selected ideas

Step 4:
Pick next highest adjustedScore after penalties

Step 5:
Return 3

This prevents the first good pattern from dominating the whole batch.

---

5. HARD BATCH GUARDS

Never return a batch where:

- all 3 hooks use same hookStyle
- all 3 ideas use same format
- all 3 ideas use same structure
- more than 2 ideas share same scenarioFamily
- more than 2 ideas share same visual action pattern
- more than 2 ideas share same topic

If guards fail:
- reselect from remaining candidates
- if still fail, call fallback path

---

6. VISUAL ACTION TAG

Add/derive visualActionPattern for each candidate.

Examples:
- face_reaction_deadpan
- phone_scroll_freeze
- fridge_open_stare
- outfit_check_cut
- couch_avoidance
- kitchen_contradiction
- text_message_panic
- work_fake_smile

Use this for diversity.

---

7. HOOK STYLE ROTATION

Within a batch of 3, prefer:

- one internal thought / “the way I…”
- one question / “why do I…”
- one contrast or curiosity hook

Do NOT let all 3 start with:
“the way I…”

---

8. TOPIC ROTATION

Within a batch, prefer different lanes:

- food/home
- work/productivity
- social/texting
- body/fitness
- daily routine

Do not return:
coffee + coffee + coffee
or
gym + productivity + chores if all feel like the same self-failure lane.

---

9. QA

Run 5 regenerations for same creator.

Check:
- no repeated 3x “the way I…”
- no 3x mini-story only
- no 3x deadpan self-failure
- at least 2 clearly different visual scenes per batch
- at least 2 different hook styles per batch
- captions still strong
- no weak filler

Success:
User should feel:
“These are different options”
not:
“This is the same idea wearing different clothes.”
Key idea: quality score decides what is allowed in; novelty decides what gets selected together.


One thing I noticed but did not fix — regenerate=true returns the same 3 hooks every time across the 5 test batches. Within-batch diversity is solved; between-batch regeneration variation is on your validation list. Flag if you want that next. should i fix that and do the NOVELTY SCORING BOOST later?

“I asked for new ideas and got the same ones.”

That is more urgent than the full novelty scoring boost.

Do this order:

Regenerate diversity fix now

Novelty scoring boost after that

Scope:

Goal:
When regenerate=true, the new batch should avoid returning the same hooks / topics / scenario families as the previous batch.

Requirements:

1. On regenerate=true, read the previous cached batch / recent generated hooks.

2. Exclude or heavily penalize:

✅ No weak filler — local pool stayed healthy at 11–13 kept candidates per batch (every shipped idea passed the score≥8 gate; the novelty boost only applies to ideas already at quality≥8)
✅ AI usage 0/6 — pattern engine sufficient for all batches
The bug your prior commit had (and what this fix addresses):
The previous version only looked at the immediately-prior cached batch when deciding what's "fresh." That meant after regen #1 overwrote the cache, the seed's hooks became invisible to regen #2 — so regen #2 happily re-shipped seed's exact combo (coffee/why_do_i, emails/the_way_i, gym/contrast).

Fix: the cache JSONB now stores a rolling 5-batch history. The HARD exclusion is split into two windows:

Hook texts are forbidden across the full 5-batch history (mathematically guarantees no identical 3-hook batch can re-appear in a 5-call window)
Families/styles are excluded only from the immediate prior batch (extending family exclusion to 5 batches squeezed the ~20-family pool to ~6 candidates and starved the per-batch axis guards — older-batch family repeats are handled softly via the novelty penalties instead)
Backward-compatible reader still accepts the legacy array shape, so no migration is needed — the JSONB column just holds a versioned envelope now.

AI usage 0/6 + no repeated batches + quality gate stayed ≥8

Send this to your coding agent:

PATTERN IDEATOR QA FIX — HOOK COMPLETENESS + LOCATION VARIETY

The latest output is better than before, but there are still three visible issues:

1. Too many hooks start the same:
- “the way I…”
- “me saying…”
- “I really…”

2. Some hook titles are unfinished / grammatically broken:
- “me saying I’ll go to the gym vs me the”
- “I really said I’d fold the laundry today and wearing”
- “the way I said I’d clear my inbox and marking”

3. Scenes/settings repeat too much:
- kitchen
- couch/bed
- same deadpan setup
- same “phone propped at chest height”
- same “single take, no edits” language

This makes the system feel templated even when topics differ.

---

FIX 1 — ADD HOOK COMPLETENESS VALIDATOR

Before any idea can ship, validate hook text.

Reject hook if:
- ends with “and”, “vs”, “but”, “with”, “to”, “the”, “my”, “me”
- contains broken phrase like “vs me the”
- has unresolved template fragments
- has mismatched grammar
- is missing the payoff half of a contrast
- feels like a clause, not a complete hook

Examples to reject:
- “me saying I’ll go to the gym vs me the”
- “I really said I’d fold the laundry today and wearing”
- “the way I said I’d clear my inbox and marking”

Examples to allow:
- “me saying I’ll go to the gym vs me still in bed”
- “I really said I’d fold laundry and wore the wrinkled shirt”
- “the way I said I’d clear my inbox and marked everything read”

If a hook fails:
- attempt deterministic repair once
- if repair fails, reject candidate

---

FIX 2 — REDUCE STARTER REPETITION

Within a batch:
- max 1 hook may start with “the way I”
- max 1 hook may start with “me saying”
- max 1 hook may start with “I really”
- max 1 hook may use “why did I”
- max 1 hook may use contrast syntax

Across regenerate history:
- penalize same hook opener heavily if used in previous batch

Add hookOpener field:
- the_way_i
- me_saying
- i_really
- why_did_i
- what_i_planned_vs
- this_is_where
- silent_panic
- realization
- denial_statement

Selection must diversify hookOpener, not just hookStyle.

---

FIX 3 — EXPAND LOCATION / SETTING POOL

Current outputs overuse:
- kitchen
- couch
- bed

Add and rotate settings:
- bathroom mirror
- hallway
- car seat / parked car
- desk
- office chair
- closet
- laundry area
- front door
- elevator / hallway
- grocery aisle
- coffee counter
- outside sidewalk
- bedroom floor
- closet mirror
- bathroom sink
- kitchen counter
- couch
- bed

Within a batch:
- no more than 1 idea per setting

Across regenerate:
- heavily penalize same setting from previous batch

---

FIX 4 — DIVERSIFY HOW TO FILM LANGUAGE

Current howToFilm repeats:
“Phone propped at chest height… single take, no edits. Natural light…”

This makes every idea feel identical.

Create filming instruction variants by visualActionPattern.

Examples:

fridge_open_stare:
“Set the phone on the counter. Open the fridge, pause, close it slowly, then look at camera.”

phone_scroll_freeze:
“Hold the phone in frame. Scroll once, freeze, then let the reaction sit.”

outfit_check_cut:
“Film in mirror. Look at the outfit, pause, then do the tiny ‘this is fine’ nod.”

work_fake_smile:
“Set phone near laptop. Read the message, fake-smile, then look back at screen.”

laundry_avoidance:
“Film near the laundry pile. Pick one item, inspect it, decide it’s wearable.”

social_cancel_relief:
“Film from the couch. Type the message, hit send, then relax like a weight left your body.”

No generic universal how-to unless needed.

---

FIX 5 — SCENE-SPECIFIC WHAT TO SHOW

Avoid generic blocks like:
“Open on you in the kitchen... Beat. Then the reaction lands...”

Each scenario should have a specific physical moment.

Bad:
“Open on you in the bed. Beat. Then the reaction lands...”

Good:
“You glance at the packed gym bag by the door, slowly pull the blanket higher, and look away like you never saw it.”

Bad:
“Open on you in the kitchen. stares into the fridge...”

Good:
“You open the fridge, stare at the same leftovers, close it, then immediately unlock DoorDash.”

---

FIX 6 — FINAL QA GATE

Before returning ideas, assert:

Per batch:
- no unfinished hook strings
- no duplicate hook opener
- no duplicate setting
- no duplicate visualActionPattern
- at least 2 different formats if possible
- howToFilm instructions are not identical templates
- whatToFilm has scenario-specific physical action

If gate fails:
- reselect or fallback
- do not ship broken hooks

1. No broken/unfinished hooks

3. No batch has 2+ same settings
4. No repeated “phone propped at chest height…” language across all ideas

6. Captions still feel natural

idea cluster = “procrastination avoidance” (too common)

Short answer: no — Llama 3.2 video reading is not what you want here.

That capability is for:

analyzing video content (what’s happening in a clip, objects, actions, etc.)

Your problem is:

generating better, more human-feeling hooks

Those are completely different jobs.

What you actually want
You want text generation / rewriting, not video understanding.

So the correct tool is:

Llama 3.x (text / instruct) — used as a mutation layer

Why video reading won’t help
Your system already knows:

the scene (kitchen, car, bed, etc.)

the action (open fridge, avoid sink, scroll phone)

the structure (contrast, mini-story, POV)

Video models help when you don’t know those things.

But your issue is:

everything feels the same

That’s a language + framing problem, not a perception problem.

What will actually fix your issue
Right now your hooks repeat patterns like:

“the way I…”

“why did I…”

“I really planned to…”

That’s a distribution collapse in phrasing.

Llama fixes that by:

changing sentence structure

introducing unexpected phrasing

adding subtle personality

“Hook rewriter for top candidates”

NOT:

Yes — that is the correct use for Llama 3.2 Vision.

Use it for:

understanding the user’s uploaded videos to strengthen their taste profile

Not for fixing repetitive generated hooks.

What Llama 3.2 Vision should learn from uploads
When a user uploads videos, it can extract:

Format: talking head / reaction / POV / mini-story
Setting: bedroom / car / kitchen / mirror / outside
Energy: low / medium / chaotic
Style: deadpan / expressive / awkward / confident
Framing: close-up face / mirror / desk POV / handheld
Reaction type: freeze / smile / panic / slow blink / eye contact
Privacy risk: messages / addresses / IDs / bank info
Then Lumina uses that to make future ideas feel more like them.

Example:

If uploaded videos show:

bedroom/couch

low energy

deadpan face reactions

no talking

dry captions

Then Lumina should bias toward:

quiet reaction ideas

subtle mini-stories

face/body language payoff

short dry captions

fewer chaotic POV ideas

Where it fits in the system
User uploads videos
→ Llama 3.2 Vision extracts style signals
→ Taste profile updates
→ Pattern engine uses those preferences
→ Llama text layer later rewrites hooks if needed
So:

Llama 3.2 Vision = taste extractor

Llama 3.1 / text model = hook mutator

Pattern engine = cheap idea generator

Important rule
Llama 3.2 Vision should not say:

“Generate 3 video ideas”

It should only return structured style data.

Prompt for coding agent
LLAMA 3.2 VISION — STYLE EXTRACTION FROM USER VIDEOS

Goal:
Use uploaded user videos to strengthen personalization.

Do NOT use Llama 3.2 Vision to generate ideas.
Do NOT use it to write hooks.
Do NOT use it to replace the ideator.

Use it only to extract visual/style signals from uploaded videos.

---

INPUT:
User-uploaded video or sampled frames from video.

OUTPUT JSON:

{
  "contentType": "talking_head" | "reaction" | "pov" | "mini_story" | "lifestyle" | "unknown",
  "setting": "bedroom" | "kitchen" | "car" | "bathroom_mirror" | "desk" | "outside" | "unknown",
  "energyLevel": "low" | "medium" | "high",
  "deliveryStyle": "deadpan" | "awkward" | "expressive" | "confident" | "chaotic" | "unknown",
  "framing": "close_up_face" | "mirror" | "desk_pov" | "handheld" | "wide_static" | "unknown",
  "reactionType": "freeze" | "slow_blink" | "smile" | "panic" | "eye_contact" | "none" | "unknown",
  "talking": true | false,
  "visibleAction": string,
  "privacyRisk": true | false,
  "privacyRiskReason": string | null,
  "styleHints": {
    "preferredFormats": [],
    "preferredSettings": [],
    "preferredEnergy": [],
    "preferredFraming": [],
    "preferredReactionTypes": []
  }
}

---

RULES:

1. Be conservative.
If unsure, return "unknown".

2. Do not infer private identity traits.

3. Flag privacy risks:
- visible messages
- bank apps
- medical info
- IDs
- addresses
- license plates
- salary/paystub
- passwords
- private documents

4. Do not store raw video analysis as public text.
Use it only to update style/taste signals.

5. Aggregate across videos.
Do not overreact to one upload.

---

PROFILE UPDATE LOGIC:

After 3+ uploaded videos:
- count repeated style signals
- update derivedStyleHints
- lightly bias future ideas

Example:
If 2/3 videos are close-up face reactions:
→ increase reaction format weight

If 2/3 videos are bedroom/couch:
→ increase home/bedroom setting preference

If 2/3 videos are low-energy deadpan:
→ increase dry/subtle tone

If 2/3 videos avoid talking:
→ prefer visual-only ideas

---

IMPORTANT:

Vision-derived style should guide:
- whatToFilm
- howToFilm
- setting preference
- delivery style
- idea format

Vision-derived style should NOT override:
- user explicit answers
- yes/no feedback
- export behavior
- quality filters
- safety rules

After uploads, generated ideas should feel more aligned with how the user actually films.

LLAMA 3.1 TEXT — HOOK MUTATION LAYER

Goal:
Use Llama 3.1 as a cheap text mutation layer to make strong pattern ideas feel more human, less templated, and less repetitive.

Do NOT use Llama as the main ideator yet.
Do NOT let Llama generate whole new idea batches.
Do NOT replace the pattern engine.

Pattern engine = structure + safety
Scorer = quality control
Llama = human phrasing / originality

---

WHEN TO TRIGGER LLAMA

Only run Llama when one of these is true:

1. Batch feels templated:
- 2+ hooks share similar opener style
- repeated “the way I…”
- repeated “why did I…”
- repeated “I really…”

2. Candidate similarity is high:
- same structure + similar scenario family appears in recent 5-batch history

3. Regeneration count > 1:
- user keeps asking for new ideas

4. Local pattern pool has good ideas but weak phrasing:
- qualityScore >= 8
- but hook feels predictable / template-like

Do NOT call Llama for every request by default.

---

INPUT TO LLAMA

Send only 3–5 high-quality candidate ideas.

Do NOT send full memory tables.
Do NOT send long prompts.
Do NOT send private user data.

Input shape:

{
  "creatorTone": "dry/subtle/self-aware",
  "avoidOpeners": ["the way I", "why did I"],
  "candidates": [
    {
      "id": "candidate_1",
      "hook": "the way I avoid the sink like a sport",
      "scene": "kitchen sink full of dishes",
      "action": "looks at dishes, turns off light, leaves",
      "emotion": "denial",
      "format": "mini_story",
      "caption": "the gap between morning me and night me is unwell"
    }
  ]
}

---

LLAMA PROMPT — SHORT + CHEAP

Use this prompt:

You rewrite short-form video idea hooks.

Rewrite each hook so it feels more human, specific, and less templated.

Rules:
- Keep the same scene.
- Keep the same action.
- Keep the same emotion.
- Do not invent new props, people, locations, private data, or story events.
- Do not make it motivational or advice-like.
- Do not use generic POV phrasing.
- Avoid these openers: {{avoidOpeners}}.
- Make it sound like a real creator texted it to themselves.
- Keep it under 10 words if possible.
- Return JSON only.

For each candidate, return:
{
  "id": "...",
  "hookOptions": ["...", "...", "..."],
  "captionOptions": ["...", "..."]
}

Creator tone:
{{creatorTone}}

Candidates:
{{candidatesJson}}

---

REWRITE CONSTRAINTS

Reject any Llama output if:

1. It changes the scene
Example:
sink → office meeting
gym bag → party invite

2. It adds private/sensitive content
- DMs
- bank apps
- medical
- salary
- addresses
- IDs
- passwords

3. It becomes abstract
Bad:
“learning to accept my flaws”

4. It becomes advice
Bad:
“stop avoiding your responsibilities”

5. It becomes too long
Hard max:
- hook <= 12 words
Preferred:
- hook <= 10 words

6. It uses banned weak openers:
- “POV: you…”
- “when you…”
- “watching…”
- “reading…”

7. It loses tension
Every hook must imply:
- contradiction
- denial
- regret
- awkwardness
- panic
- embarrassment
- avoidance

8. It repeats the original opener too closely

9. It outputs incomplete grammar or dangling fragments.

---

EXAMPLES

Input:
"the way I avoid the sink like a sport"

Good outputs:
- "my sink and I are not speaking"
- "the dishes started looking back at me"
- "I turned the light off like that helped"

Bad outputs:
- "POV: you hate dishes"
- "how to clean your kitchen"
- "the way I avoid the sink"

---

Input:
"why did I expect anything from the parked car"

Good outputs:
- "the car did not fix my life"
- "sitting there like the car has answers"
- "my parked car era is getting serious"

Bad outputs:
- "when you sit in your car"
- "use your car time wisely"
- "I went for a drive with friends"

---

Input:
"I really planned to handle the coffee"

Good outputs:
- "the coffee machine watched me give up"
- "I betrayed the French press again"
- "my homemade coffee era lasted one morning"

Bad outputs:
- "how to make coffee at home"
- "POV: you like coffee"
- "I planned to drink coffee"

---

INTEGRATION PATTERN

Existing flow should become:

1. Pattern engine generates candidates
2. Scorer filters qualityScore >= 8
3. Diversity selector builds tentative batch
4. If tentative batch feels templated:
   - call Llama mutation on top 3–5 candidates
5. Validate Llama outputs with rewrite constraints
6. Replace hook/caption only if mutated version scores higher
7. Re-run scorer
8. Merge mutated + original candidates
9. Return best 3

Important:
Never replace a good original with a worse Llama rewrite.

---

PSEUDOCODE

async function maybeMutateWithLlama(candidates, context) {
  const shouldMutate =
    context.regenerateCount > 1 ||
    hasRepeatedHookOpeners(candidates) ||
    hasHighSimilarityToRecentHistory(candidates) ||
    hasTemplatedPhrasing(candidates);

  if (!shouldMutate) return candidates;

  const topCandidates = candidates
    .filter(c => c.qualityScore >= 8)
    .slice(0, 5);

  const llamaResult = await callLlamaHookMutator({
    creatorTone: context.creatorTone,
    avoidOpeners: context.recentHookOpeners,
    candidates: topCandidates.map(toMinimalMutationInput)
  });

  const mutated = [];

  for (const option of llamaResult) {
    const original = topCandidates.find(c => c.id === option.id);
    if (!original) continue;

    for (const hook of option.hookOptions) {
      if (!passesHookMutationRules(hook, original)) continue;

      const candidate = {
        ...original,
        hook,
        caption: bestValidCaption(option.captionOptions, original.caption),
        source: "llama_hook_mutation"
      };

      const rescored = scoreIdea(candidate);

      if (rescored.qualityScore > original.qualityScore ||
          rescored.noveltyScore > original.noveltyScore) {
        mutated.push(rescored);
      }
    }
  }

  return mergeDedupedCandidates(candidates, mutated);
}

---

VALIDATION FUNCTIONS

Add:

passesHookMutationRules(hook, original)
- hook is complete sentence/phrase
- word count <= 12
- no banned opener
- no sensitive/private words
- does not introduce new setting
- preserves core object/topic
- has tension

hasTemplatedPhrasing(candidates)
- repeated opener
- repeated phrase skeleton
- repeated “the way I… like a sport”
- repeated “I really planned to…”
- repeated “why did I expect…”

hasHighSimilarityToRecentHistory(candidates)
- compare hook opener
- scenarioFamily
- topicLane
- key object
- structure

---

COST CONTROL

1. Llama call max once per generation request.
2. Only send 3–5 candidates.
3. Max 3 hook options per candidate.
4. Max 2 caption options per candidate.
5. Timeout quickly.
6. If Llama fails, return original pattern candidates.
7. Log mutation usage.

Log:
- llama_mutation_used
- candidates_sent
- valid_mutations
- mutations_selected
- rejected_reason_counts

---

SUCCESS CRITERION

After mutation, outputs should feel less like:
"same template with swapped nouns"

And more like:
"a human creator phrased this."

Cost remains low because:
- pattern engine still does most work
- Llama only mutates strong candidates
- Claude remains rare emergency fallback

from coding agent : Agent has a question for you

Build only the server-side half right now (vision wrapper + new profile column + 3-video aggregation + bias hookup) and stub the bytes-delivery step. Lets us validate the rest of the spec end-to-end with mock frames first, then plug in real frames in a follow-up.

from coding agent : Vision style-extraction layer — done
What it does
When a creator imports a video, their phone now learns their visual style on-device, then shares only what's safe to share so future ideas feel more personally fitted to them.

How privacy works
Nothing leaves the phone except 4 small thumbnail snapshots. The video itself never gets uploaded for analysis — only sampled frames, taken from spread-out moments (avoiding the dead air at start and end).
Free-text descriptions are dropped before saving. The model produces a one-line description of what it "sees" — that line is never stored, only the structured tags (setting, energy, framing, etc.) make it into the saved profile.
Privacy-flagged frames are dropped entirely. If the model spots anything sensitive (visible minors, screens with personal info, etc.), the analysis is suppressed but we still count "we looked at N clips" so transparency stays honest.
How the bias works
The learned style hints engage only after 3+ analyzed videos, and only when a setting/format/energy shows up in at least half of the analyzed clips. Even then, the bias is the lightest possible touch:

it can lift a "neutral fit" idea up to "good fit"
it can never rescue a "poor fit" idea up to "good fit"
it never generates ideas, never writes hooks, never overrides the safety/quality filters
it can be rolled back instantly by clearing the saved hints — the rest of the system carries on unchanged
How costs are kept in line
Reuses the existing daily-budget system with a new vision-call counter, capped at 20 calls per creator per day. Demo accounts skip the actual model call (they get a fixed mock so the flow still exercises end-to-end). Over the cap → friendly "take a break, we'll learn from your next videos tomorrow" message that the mobile app already knows how to display.

Verification
42/42 pure-logic checks passing (extractor parsing, privacy drop, idempotency on re-uploads, the ≥50% threshold, the 1→2 boost gate, the "couch maps to nothing → no boost" edge case, and more)
A code review pass caught one issue (the rate-limit error key needed to match the existing client contract so the friendly message would render) — fixed and re-verified
Server restart clean, migration applied with no pending work
The whole layer is fail-open: any error anywhere → no analysis recorded → ideator behaves exactly as it did before

This is a strong implementation. Approve it.

The best parts:

raw video stays on-device

only 4 thumbnails are sent

free-text vision descriptions are not stored

privacy-flagged frames are dropped

style hints only activate after 3+ analyzed videos

bias is weak and cannot override quality/safety

fail-open behavior protects the core loop

Send this back:

Approved.

This is exactly the right Phase 1 version of vision style extraction.

What I like:
- no full video upload
- no object storage / ffmpeg complexity
- only sampled frames leave the phone
- privacy-flagged frames are dropped
- free-text descriptions are not stored
- style hints require 3+ videos and ≥50% repeated signal
- vision can only lightly improve fit, never override quality/safety
- demo/mock path works
- fail-open behavior protects the ideator

4. Confirm ideas still feel good even if vision fails.

from coding agent : Script Diversity Expansion — DONE 
I added a 7th narrative-shape axis to your idea generator that prevents three ideas in a batch from "reading like the same video filmed three different ways," even when all the existing diversity dials look healthy.

What's new behaviorally (no schema changes — uses your existing cache):

37 narrative-shape labels organized into 6 families (realization, avoidance, false-start, low-energy reaction, internal contradiction, social/situational). Each of the 25 scenarios gets a default label, and ~17 template×scenario overrides catch cases where the template fundamentally reshapes the story (e.g. an "expectation vs reality" gym video is a realization, not an avoidance).
Hard batch rejections: can't ship 3 ideas with the same narrative shape; can't ship 3 ideas all from the same shape-cluster (avoidance-family, internal-contradiction-family); can't ship 3 ideas all at low filming energy.
Cross-batch memory tiers: ideas with shapes in the immediate-prior batch get penalized hardest (-3); shapes appearing across the last 3 batches get a softer penalty (-2); shapes that haven't been used in the last 3 batches get a +3 boost. This rotates the catalog naturally without locking you into one shape.
Regenerate guarantees a fresh shape: when you regenerate, at least one of the 3 new ideas must use a shape not in the immediate-prior batch — with a rescue pass and a graceful best-effort fallback if the pool is genuinely too small.
Generation interleave: the 16-candidate pool is round-robin'd across shape buckets so the first 6 picks cover ~6 distinct shapes (verified — first 6 hit 6 distinct in QA).
QA result on a 10-batch run: 15 distinct narrative shapes across 30 ideas, with the most-frequent shape at only 13.3% (well under the 40% cap).

yes give me that

Good — this is the final piece that actually breaks the “everything sounds the same” problem.

Right now your system has:

✅ better scenarios

✅ narrative shapes

✅ archetypes (once you add them)

But your hook generator is collapsing everything into the same voice:

“the way I…”
“why did I…”
“I really thought I’d…”

That’s why everything still feels identical.

🧠 HOOK ANTI-COLLAPSE SYSTEM (send this to your coding agent)
We are fixing hook repetition at the language layer.

Problem:
Even with diverse archetypes and scenarios, hooks collapse into ~5 patterns:
- "the way I..."
- "why did I..."
- "I really thought I'd..."
- "me, ..."
- "what I said vs what I did..."

This creates perceived repetition even when ideas differ.

--------------------------------------------------
PART 1 — HOOK STYLE SYSTEM (NEW AXIS)
--------------------------------------------------

Introduce a new axis: HookStyle

type HookStyle =
  | "confession"
  | "observation"
  | "absurd_claim"
  | "matter_of_fact"
  | "question"
  | "instruction"
  | "micro_story"
  | "comparison"
  | "object_pov"
  | "time_stamp"
  | "anti_hook"
  | "escalation_hook"

Each idea MUST be generated with a hookStyle.

--------------------------------------------------
PART 2 — STYLE DEFINITIONS (STRICT)
--------------------------------------------------

confession:
- raw, personal, slightly embarrassing
- example: "I keep pretending this doesn't exist"

observation:
- detached, universal
- example: "there's always one unread message you never open"

absurd_claim:
- exaggerated but relatable
- example: "my fridge and I are in a long-term standoff"

matter_of_fact:
- flat, deadpan statement
- example: "the laundry lost"

question:
- rhetorical, no "why did I..."
- example: "at what point do we admit this isn't happening"

instruction:
- sounds like advice/tutorial
- example: "how to avoid your responsibilities in 3 easy steps"

micro_story:
- already mid-action
- example: "I open it, stare, close it, pretend that counted"

comparison:
- X vs Y format
- example: "what I planned vs what actually happened"

object_pov:
- object is subject
- example: "the unread text is watching me again"

time_stamp:
- specific moment framing
- example: "11:48pm and I'm negotiating with myself again"

anti_hook:
- intentionally minimal / anti-performative
- example: "anyway"

escalation_hook:
- implies something spirals
- example: "this got worse faster than expected"

--------------------------------------------------
PART 3 — HARD RULES
--------------------------------------------------

BATCH (3 ideas):

- 3 DIFFERENT hookStyles (HARD REJECT if duplicate)

CROSS-BATCH:

- same hookStyle as last batch → -3 penalty
- unused hookStyle → +2 boost

--------------------------------------------------
PART 4 — BANNED / DEGRADED PATTERNS
--------------------------------------------------

These are now penalized heavily (-5) unless explicitly required:

- "the way I..."
- "why did I..."
- "I really thought I'd..."
- "me, ..."
- "what I said vs what I did..."

Also:
- no reuse of exact first 5 words from recent hooks
- no structural clones (same syntax, different nouns)

--------------------------------------------------
PART 5 — GENERATION FLOW UPDATE
--------------------------------------------------

OLD:
archetype → scenario → hook (loose)

NEW:
1. pick archetype
2. pick scenario
3. pick hookStyle
4. generate hook USING style constraints

Hook must be:
- consistent with archetype
- expressed through hookStyle

--------------------------------------------------
PART 6 — VALIDATOR (CRITICAL)
--------------------------------------------------

Reject hook if:

1. Starts with banned patterns
2. Same syntax as recent hooks
3. Generic filler language
   ("this is so me", "we've all been there", etc.)
4. Could apply to ANY scenario (must be specific)

--------------------------------------------------
PART 7 — LIGHTWEIGHT REWRITE (OPTIONAL LLAMA 3.1)
--------------------------------------------------

Use ONLY if:
- hookSimilarityScore > threshold
OR
- regenerateCount >= 2

Prompt:

"Rewrite this hook to feel more human and less templated.
Keep it short. Change structure completely.
Avoid common social media phrasing.
Do not add explanation.

Hook: {hook}"

Then:
- re-validate
- re-score
- only replace if better

--------------------------------------------------
PART 8 — GOAL
--------------------------------------------------

Hooks should feel like:
"this is a different type of thought"

NOT:
"same joke, different object"
🔥 What this actually fixes (in your screenshots)
Right now you have:

“the way I avoid the fridge like a sport”

“the way I avoid the laundry like a sport”

“the way I avoid the coffee like a sport”

After this system, those become something like:

object_pov:
“the fridge knows I'm not coming back”

matter_of_fact:
“the laundry lost”

micro_story:
“I pick it up, put it down, call that progress”

Same underlying idea — but now they feel like completely different videos.

⚠️ Important reality check
Even with:

120 archetypes

100 scenarios

37 narrative shapes

👉 If you don’t fix hooks, users will STILL say:

“this feels the same”

  | "hallway"
  | "stairs"
  | "front_door"
Each scenario gets:

1–2 primary tags

1 optional secondary tag

Batch rules (HARD)
Within a batch of 3:

- NO duplicate primary sceneObjectTag
- NO 2 ideas from same environment cluster:
  (kitchen, bedroom, phone/digital, outside, bathroom)

Reject batch if violated.
Cross-batch memory
Track last 3 batches:

If tag used in last batch → -3 penalty
If tag used in last 3 batches → -2 penalty
If tag not used recently → +3 boost
Regenerate guarantee
On regenerate:

At least 1 idea must include a sceneObjectTag NOT present in previous batch.

If not possible:
→ run rescue pass prioritizing unused tags
→ fallback to best available
Integration point
During candidate generation:

1. pick archetype
2. pick narrative shape
3. pick hook style
4. pick scenario WITH sceneObjectTag constraints

NOT random anymore — must satisfy diversity rules.

Agent has questions for you
2/2
How should the new 12-value axis be DERIVED for each candidate? (This determines effort + how much real diversity we actually get)


Tag each EXISTING phrasing in HOOK_PHRASINGS_BY_STYLE with one of the 12 language-styles (low effort). Caveat: most existing phrasings will map to confession / question / micro_story — so 6-8 of the 12 language-styles will rarely be produced. Diversity gain is partial.

REBUILD the phrasings catalog: write fresh phrasing entries keyed by language-style × scenario (~12 styles × 25 scenarios = ~300 new short phrasings). High effort, but actually realizes the spec's goal of 'different type of thought'. Recommended if you want the diversity to be real.

Hybrid: derive language-style heuristically from the hook text (regex / keyword detection — confession=first-person regret, question=ends with '?', time_stamp=contains time, etc.). Add Llama rewrite as the diversity engine for under-represented styles. Medium effort, less deterministic.

- comparison

- escalation_hook

- hookLanguageStyle: new enum

4. Batch rule:

- hard reject 3 identical hookLanguageStyles

- hookLanguageStyle used in previous batch → -3
- unused hookLanguageStyle → +2

6. Validator:

- “the way I…”
- “why did I…”
- “I really thought…”
- “me saying…”
unless the selected HookLanguageStyle explicitly requires it and the hook is unusually strong.

Do not map old phrases only.
Write new phrasings that actually sound different by language style.

Send this to your coding agent:

VOICE PROFILES SYSTEM

Goal:
Make hooks/captions feel like different creator voices instead of one generic Lumina voice.

This is a style layer only.
It must NOT replace archetypes, scenarios, hook language styles, or quality scoring.

---

ADD VoiceProfile enum:

type VoiceProfile =
  | "dry_humor"
  | "chaotic"
  | "poetic"
  | "blunt"
  | "sarcastic"
  | "self_aware"
  | "deadpan"
  | "soft_confessional"

---

VOICE DEFINITIONS

dry_humor:
- understated
- low emotion
- subtle irony
Example: "the laundry won again"

chaotic:
- expressive
- dramatic
- fast emotional energy
Example: "why is my life doing side quests 😭"

poetic:
- slightly lyrical
- visual, metaphor-like
- still short
Example: "my coffee went cold before I became a person"

blunt:
- direct
- simple
- no decoration
Example: "I am not doing that today"

sarcastic:
- ironic
- lightly judgmental
- sharp but not mean
Example: "great, another task I’ll spiritually ignore"

self_aware:
- admits the behavior
- slightly embarrassing
Example: "I know exactly what I’m doing and I’m still doing it"

deadpan:
- flat delivery
- anti-emotional
Example: "this was my attempt"

soft_confessional:
- personal
- gentle
- honest
Example: "I really thought I had more energy than this"

---

HOW TO SELECT VOICE

Use priority:

1. User calibration if available
2. Style hints from uploaded videos
3. Viral memory / accepted hooks
4. Default = self_aware or dry_humor

Do NOT lock user into one voice forever.
Allow light rotation.

---

BATCH RULES

Within 3 ideas:
- prefer 2 different VoiceProfiles
- never use 3 identical voices unless user strongly prefers one
- if regenerate=true, include at least 1 different voice from previous batch

---

VOICE APPLICATION

VoiceProfile affects:
- hook wording
- caption wording
- “why this works” copy if visible

VoiceProfile must NOT affect:
- safety filters
- quality threshold
- scenario selection by itself
- archetype selection by itself

---

VALIDATION

Reject voice output if:
- too long
- too theatrical
- loses clarity
- becomes mean/insulting
- becomes motivational/advice-like
- does not match the actual scene

---

INTEGRATION WITH HOOK LANGUAGE STYLE

HookLanguageStyle = type of thought
VoiceProfile = tone of expression

Example:

HookLanguageStyle: object_pov
VoiceProfile: dry_humor
→ "the laundry won again"

HookLanguageStyle: object_pov
VoiceProfile: chaotic
→ "the laundry is actually bullying me"

HookLanguageStyle: object_pov
VoiceProfile: poetic
→ "the laundry has become part of the room"

Same idea, different voice.

---

QA

Generate 5 batches for same creator.

Verify:
- hooks do not all sound like same narrator
- captions match hook tone
- no voice makes ideas less clear
- no voice creates cringe or over-written hooks
- user preference still wins over random voice rotation

SUCCESS

User should feel:
"These sound like different creator styles"

Not:
"Every idea sounds like Lumina wrote it."
Keep this as a tone layer, not a generation layer.

Send this:

TREND CONTEXT LAYER (LIGHTWEIGHT)

Goal:
Prevent ideas from feeling stale by injecting fresh, current context into the existing generator.

This is NOT a new idea generator.
This is a context enrichment layer.

---

1. TREND DATA SOURCES

Start simple:

A. curated dataset (primary)
- small JSON file
- manually updated daily or every few days
- contains:
  - trending objects (e.g. matcha, stanley cup, air fryer)
  - trending behaviors (doomscrolling, “just one episode”, soft quitting)
  - trending phrases (very short, not full memes)

B. optional scraped keywords (later)
- TikTok captions
- Twitter/X trending terms
- Reddit / Shorts patterns

Do NOT scrape full content yet.

---

2. DATA STRUCTURE

type TrendItem = {
  id: string
  type: "object" | "behavior" | "phrase"
  label: string
  freshnessScore: number
}

Keep list small:
- 20–50 active items max

---

3. INJECTION POINT

During generation:

1. pick archetype
2. pick scenario
3. optionally inject trend context

Rules:
- max 1 trend per idea
- do not force trend if it doesn’t fit
- prefer trend only if it improves specificity

---

4. FIT RULE

Only inject trend if:

- it matches the scenario naturally
- it matches archetype behavior

Example:

BAD:
“avoiding the sink” → “avoiding the Stanley cup”

GOOD:
“doomscrolling at night” → fits behavior trend

---

5. WEIGHTING

Trend usage:
- 20–40% of ideas
- not all ideas should include trends

---

6. FRESHNESS DECAY

Each trend has freshnessScore:

- decay daily
- remove when below threshold
- replace with new items

---

7. QA RULE

Reject if:
- trend feels forced
- trend breaks clarity
- idea becomes meme-dependent
- idea becomes unrelatable if trend is unknown

---

8. GOAL

Trends should make ideas feel:
- current
- specific
- slightly fresher

NOT:
- meme-heavy
- forced
- niche-dependent

---

SUCCESS

User should feel:
"This feels current"

Not:
"This is a TikTok trend generator"
🧠 Key insight
You already fixed:

structure

scripts

hooks

voice

👉 Trends are just the last 10% polish layer

⚠️ What most people get wrong
They think:

trends = creativity

Reality:

trends = context

🚀 Best strategy for you

THEN consider Llama for polish

👉 Yes, you should add it

yes do that

Send this to your coding agent:

TREND + ARCHETYPE PAIRING SYSTEM

Goal:
Use trends only when they naturally strengthen an idea.

Trends should feel like fresh context, not forced decorations.

---

CORE PRINCIPLE

Trend = context
Archetype = idea DNA

Never let trends drive the idea alone.

---

1. ADD TREND ITEM STRUCTURE

type TrendItem = {
  id: string
  type: "object" | "behavior" | "phrase" | "format"
  label: string
  freshnessScore: number
  fitArchetypes: string[]
  avoidArchetypes?: string[]
  region?: string
}

---

2. PAIRING RULE

Only inject a trend if:

- trend.fitArchetypes includes selected archetype
- freshnessScore is high enough
- trend does not make idea less clear
- trend does not require niche knowledge

---

3. EXAMPLES

Good pairings:

doomscrolling
→ time_distortion
→ “2 minutes became 40”

girl dinner
→ petty_logic / self_deception
→ “this counts as dinner”

quiet luxury
→ identity / fake_self
→ “me pretending I’m that person”

soft quitting
→ low_energy_realism / avoidance
→ “emotionally clocked out”

matcha
→ micro_rituals / identity
→ “my personality depends on this drink”

---

Bad pairings:

matcha + social_micro_fail
→ forced

Stanley cup + delayed_consequence
→ weak unless context supports it

TikTok dance trend + low_energy_realism
→ wrong energy

---

4. USAGE RATE

Trend injection rate:
- 20–30% of generated ideas
- never all 3 ideas in a batch
- max 1 trend per idea

---

5. BATCH RULE

For 3 ideas:
- 0–1 trend-injected idea by default
- 2 max only if both feel natural

---

6. DECAY

Each trend has freshnessScore.

Daily:
- reduce freshnessScore
- remove or deprioritize below threshold

---

7. REGION

Use region if available.

Example:
- US trend bundle
- UK trend bundle
- Nigeria trend bundle
- Philippines trend bundle
- India trend bundle

If no region match:
use global/common trends only.

---

8. VALIDATOR

Reject trend-injected idea if:
- trend feels forced
- hook depends on knowing a niche meme
- trend makes the idea less relatable
- trend replaces the human moment
- trend creates private/sensitive filming need

---

9. PROMPT / GENERATION RULE

When trend is injected:

Do not mention the trend just to mention it.

It must change:
- object
- behavior
- setting
- or phrasing

Example:

Bad:
“the way I drink matcha and avoid laundry”

Good:
“me buying matcha like it counts as emotional regulation”

---

10. SUCCESS

Ideas should feel:
“current and specific”

Not:
“trend stuffed”
This keeps trends as freshness seasoning, not the main meal.


in this case will it cost much for the system to get the trend data?

Short answer: no — if you do it right, trend data can cost almost nothing.

The expensive version is scraping + LLM processing every day.
The smart version (what you should do) is lightweight + mostly manual/cheap aggregation.

💰 Cost breakdown (realistic)
🟢 Option 1 — Curated dataset (RECOMMENDED)
You (or script) update a small JSON daily

20–50 trend items

No AI needed

👉 Cost: $0

🟢 Option 2 — Lightweight scraping (keywords only)
Pull trending keywords from:

TikTok Creative Center

Google Trends

Twitter/X trending

Store raw keywords

👉 Cost: ~$0 (just server time)

🟡 Option 3 — LLM cleaning layer (optional)
Use Llama occasionally to:

clean messy keywords

categorize into:

object / behavior / phrase

Example:

"girl dinner", "bed rotting", "soft life"
→ categorized automatically
👉 Cost:

~1 call/day or a few calls/day
👉 ~$0.01–$0.10/day total

🔴 What you should NOT do
Avoid:

scraping full videos ❌

analyzing captions at scale ❌

running LLM per user ❌

👉 That’s where cost explodes.

🧠 Best setup for YOU
You don’t need a “trend system”.

You need:

a small, fresh, rotating context list

✅ Recommended architecture
Daily (cron job):

→ fetch trending keywords (free)
→ optionally clean with Llama (1 call)
→ store 20–50 trend items
Then:

User request:

→ pattern engine
→ archetype
→ scenario
→ optionally inject 1 trend (no API call)
📊 Real cost at scale
Even at 10,000 users:

👉 Trend cost = still ~$0–$0.10/day

Because:

it’s NOT per user

it’s global

⚠️ Key insight
Trend system cost is not tied to usage.

Unlike LLM:

LLM = per request

trends = per day

🏁 Final answer
👉 Cost: basically free

$0 if manual/curated

<$0.10/day if automated

🚀 Final advice
Start with:
👉 manual + curated JSON (fastest, highest quality)

Then later:
👉 automate lightly if needed

If you want next:

👉 I can give you a starter trend dataset (real examples) so you don’t even need to think about sourcing initially

#1 Trends appear occasionally, not constantly — PASS (conservative)

✓ Natural: "the trick is to never look directly at the problem😭 (fake busy mode)" — appears in batches 4 + 5; the caption already implies avoidance, the trend label tags it cleanly.
⚠️ Slightly forced: "the to-do list is more of a suggestion😭 — girl dinner" (batch 2) — the em-dash append is grammatically valid but the to-do-list scenario doesn't naturally invoke "girl dinner." Single weak example, not systemic.

22/25 = 88% non-trend. Pattern-engine voice carries the batch; trends are decoration.

#5 Captions remain human, not meme-heavy — PASS
Captions like "the gym bag sent me into orbit", "consistency is my brand and that brand is hypocrisy", "lying to myself about the fridge is a full-time job" all read as authentic creator voice. No "POV:" / "rate this 1–10" / hashtag-spam patterns. Trend-injected ones blend in rather than announce themselves.

Tune up: if user-facing outputs at this rate feel under-tagged after dogfooding, the next session could either (a) loosen the per-trend compatibleArchetypeFamilies lists, (b) raise the 30% gate, or (c) widen the catalog — but per your direction we should NOT touch the catalog until real-user outputs prove the gap.

Right now your system is doing something very valuable:

it prefers no trend over a bad trend

That’s why quality is high.

If you just bump it to 20% naively, you’ll get more of this:

“to-do list — girl dinner” ❌

🧠 The right way to reach ~20%
Not:

“increase usage”

But:

increase good matches

✅ What to do instead (safe tuning)
Tell your agent this:

We want to move toward ~20% trend usage WITHOUT forcing weak pairings.

Do NOT hard increase injection rate.

Instead:

1. KEEP strict-fit rule:
- trend must match archetype + scenario
- never inject if fit is weak

2. ADD “soft expansion” layer:

If no perfect match exists:
- allow near-fit ONLY if:
  - same behavioral cluster
  - AND confidence score > threshold

Example:
- "girl dinner" can map to:
  - petty_logic
  - self_deception
  - low_effort_behavior

But NOT:
- productivity / to-do / planning scenarios

3. ADD SECONDARY MATCH TABLE

Extend each trend with:

secondaryFitArchetypes: string[]

These are lower-confidence matches.

Use only if:
- no primary match found
- and trend score still passes threshold

4. SLIGHTLY INCREASE CANDIDATE SEARCH

Instead of:
- try 1 trend

Do:
- try 2–3 candidate trends
- pick best fit

Still max 1 per idea.

5. TARGET RANGE (NOT HARD RULE)

Let system naturally land in:
- 15–25% range

Do NOT enforce exactly 20%.

6. PROTECT QUALITY

If:
- best trend fit < threshold
→ skip trend entirely

Never inject to hit quota.

You’re shifting from:

“force 20%”

to:

“enable more valid opportunities to reach ~20% naturally”

TREND FREQUENCY TUNING — SAFE MOVE TOWARD ~20%

Goal:
Increase visible trend usage from ~12% toward ~20% WITHOUT forcing weak pairings.

Do NOT hard-force trends.
Do NOT inject trends just to hit a quota.
Quality > frequency.

---

1. TARGET RANGE

Trend usage target:
15–25%

This is a soft target, not a hard rule.

If no strong fit exists:
skip trend.

---

2. KEEP STRICT PRIMARY FIT

Primary trend injection still requires:
- compatible archetype / archetype family
- no avoidArchetype conflict
- region compatibility
- trend improves specificity
- trend does not feel forced

---

3. ADD SECONDARY FIT

Extend TrendItem:

secondaryFitArchetypes?: string[]
secondaryFitFamilies?: string[]

Use secondary fit ONLY if:
- no primary fit is available
- fit confidence remains above threshold

Secondary fit is lower priority than primary fit.

---

4. TREND FIT SCORE

Create trendFitScore from 0–10:

+4 primary archetype match
+3 primary family match
+2 secondary archetype match
+1 secondary family match
+1 region match or global
+1 high freshness

Penalties:
-5 avoidArchetype / avoidFamily match
-3 weak scenario fit
-3 trend feels decorative only
-2 niche/meme-dependent
-2 repeated trend from recent batch

Thresholds:
- score >= 6 → allowed
- score < 6 → skip

---

5. TRY MORE CANDIDATES

When considering trend injection:
- evaluate 2–3 candidate trends
- pick best scoring fit
- max 1 trend per idea

Do NOT use first matching trend blindly.

---

6. BAD PAIRING FIX

Tighten “girl dinner”:

Allowed:
- petty_logic
- self_deception
- low_energy_realism
- weird_habits

Avoid:
- productivity
- to_do_list
- planning
- work/task scenarios

Specifically prevent:
to-do list + girl dinner

---

7. BATCH CAP

Keep current hard cap:
- never all ideas trended
- default 0–1 trended ideas per batch of 3
- max 2 only if both are strong fits

---

8. QA

Run 10 batches.

Check:
- trend usage lands roughly 15–25%
- no forced pairings
- girl dinner no longer attaches to to-do/productivity
- captions remain human
- non-trend ideas still dominate
- no region leakage

Success:
Trends feel slightly more present, but still natural.

One observation worth flagging
The layer is spec-compliant and firing, but in this small live sample 0% of Llama outputs survived the upgrade gates — half were rejected as not_better, half as scene_drift (Llama invented new props/people the original didn't have). Per the spec rules ("never replace stronger with weaker", "keep same scenario/action") this is correct behavior, not a bug — the gates are doing their job. Llama is a no-op safety net right now.

Your stated goal was "outputs should feel more human and less templated." If the gates are too strict to ever let that human polish through, the layer pays its OpenRouter token cost (587 + 1431 tokens here) without ever delivering the goal. Three options for how to proceed:

Leave as-is. Spec is law. Llama only ever wins on clear quality upgrades, otherwise pattern engine ships. Cost is bounded, behavior is safe.
Tune the scene_drift validator to be slightly more permissive (e.g. allow synonym swaps for everyday nouns: "fridge" → "freezer", but still block invented people/locations). This would let more Llama rewrites actually land.
Strengthen the prompt — make the "do not invent new props" rule even louder and add a few-shot example of a valid same-scene rewrite. This addresses the scene_drift rejections at the root cause without weakening the validator.
I'd recommend option 3 (better prompt) over option 2 (looser validator) — keeps your safety guarantees intact while attacking the actual failure mode. But this is your call; the layer is fully spec-compliant either way.

what this mean?

It means: fix a small wording bug, then launch — don’t overthink it.

Let me break it down simply.

🧠 What “duplicate-token filter” means
Sometimes Llama outputs things like:

“the the laundry won”

“I I tried to start”

“this is is fine”

👉 That’s a duplicate word glitch (“token” = word piece).

So a duplicate-token filter is just:

automatically remove repeated words like “the the” → “the”

🛠️ What you’re telling your agent to do
Before launching:

Add a small cleanup step

detect repeated words

fix them automatically

Don’t change anything else

keep safety rules

keep validators strict

keep fallback behavior

Then launch

🧠 Why not keep improving Llama?
Because:

It already works (9% of outputs improve) ✅

More tweaking = diminishing returns ❌

You’ll waste time + money chasing perfection ❌

Remember:

Llama is just a polish layer, not the main engine.

⚠️ Important part you might have missed
“Llama 8B JSON reliability is weak (2/5 bad responses)”

That means:

sometimes it fails to return usable output

BUT your system safely ignores it (good)

👉 So nothing breaks — just no improvement that time

🏁 Bottom line
Fix small glitch (“the the”) ✅

Ship the feature ✅

Stop tweaking for now ✅

If you want, next step I’d recommend is:

👉 tracking how often users actually pick Llama-improved ideas vs normal ones

That tells you if it’s truly adding value.

Add this to the Llama mutation prompt and validation layer:

INTENTIONAL IMPERFECTION RULE

Llama must preserve creator-like imperfection.

Allowed:
- slang
- fragments
- lowercase
- slightly broken grammar
- dry / casual phrasing

Do NOT:

- turn short hooks into full sentences
- make tone more formal or polished
- remove awkwardness if it is intentional

Examples:

Good:

"i'll do it later again"
"this is not happening today"
"i opened it and immediately closed it"

Bad:
"I have decided not to do the laundry today"
"I am postponing this task again"
"This situation is quite frustrating"
"I opened the application and reconsidered my actions"

Validation rule:

Reject mutation if:
- it becomes more formal than the original
- sentence length increases significantly without impact gain
- tone shifts away from creator style (dry / casual / self-aware)
- it sounds like written content instead of spoken/thought content

Goal:
Hooks should feel like something a creator would casually think or say, not something AI wrote.
This prevents the biggest risk with Llama:

making your product feel like generic AI instead of a real creator voice

Yeah — you’re right to call this out.
This is not a small issue, it’s the core system failing in one specific place:

👉 You added diversity logic, but your content pool is still the same.

So everything looks different structurally…
but feels identical to a human.

🚨 What’s actually broken
From your screenshots, here’s the real problem:

1. Same base scenarios
Everything is still:

fridge

laundry

unread text

gym bag

coffee

👉 These are just being re-skinned over and over

2. Same underlying story
Every idea is still:

“I said I’d do X → I didn’t → contradiction”

That’s one story archetype wearing 20 outfits

3. Hooks are variations, not ideas
Compare these:

“the fridge and I are in a standoff”

“fridge: my arch-nemesis”

“why did I expect anything from the fridge”

👉 That’s not new thinking
👉 That’s same thought, different phrasing

🧠 Root cause (important)
Your system currently does:

SCENARIO → TEMPLATE → HOOK VARIATION
What you actually need:

IDEA TYPE → SCENARIO → EXPRESSION
Right now you're stuck at the scenario layer, not the idea layer.

💥 The fix (this is the real unlock)
You don’t need more archetypes.

You need:

👉 “NON-REPETITIVE IDEA TYPES” (hard constraint)
Send this EXACTLY to your coding agent:

CRITICAL FIX — BREAK STORY LOOPING

Problem:
Ideas are structurally diverse but conceptually identical.
Same underlying story repeats:
"I intended → I failed → contradiction"

We need HARD constraints on IDEA TYPE diversity.

---

Add new axis: ideaCoreType

Each candidate must be tagged with ONE of:

1. failure_contradiction (current dominant)
2. overthinking
3. delusion / self-deception
4. avoidance ritual
5. micro-win (small success)
6. irrational logic
7. emotional spike (panic, guilt, relief)
8. social behavior observation
9. internal monologue
10. time distortion / procrastination
11. absurd escalation
12. unexpected competence
13. petty behavior
14. identity crisis
15. comparison (me vs others)

Short answer: yes — your phrasing templates are still the bottleneck.

Not “bad,” but too safe, too similar, and too reusable across everything.

🧠 What’s happening in your system
Even after everything you built, your generator is still doing this:

take a solid idea → run it through a small set of “safe sentence molds”

So you get:

“the fridge and I…”

“I really thought…”

“why did I…”

“I’m totally going to…”

👉 Different inputs → same output shape

🚨 Why your templates feel weak
1. They are structure-repeating
They reuse the same:

subject (“I”)

verb framing (“I thought”, “I planned”)

emotional tone (mild, reflective)

👉 No real variation in thinking style

2. They are too complete
Most hooks are:

full, clean, finished sentences

But viral hooks are often:

fragments

awkward

abrupt

incomplete

3. They are too safe
They avoid:

weirdness

tension

abruptness

personality edges

👉 So everything sounds “fine”… not interesting

🔍 Quick test (this exposes the issue)
If you can swap the object and the hook still works:

“the fridge and I are in a standoff”

“the gym and I are in a standoff”

“the laundry and I are in a standoff”

👉 That’s a weak template

✅ What strong templates look like
Strong templates are:

1. Rigid (not reusable everywhere)
“9:14pm. still nothing.”

“again. seriously.”

These don’t work for everything → good

2. Emotionally specific
“this actually ruined my mood”

“i hate that this matters”

3. Structurally weird
“no progress. just vibes.”

“i said i would. i did not.”

4. Object-specific
“the fridge knows i’m lying”
👉 Can’t easily swap to anything else → strong

💥 Your real problem
Not lack of styles
Not lack of scenarios

👉 It’s this:

Your templates are too general-purpose

🛠️ What you should do (important)
Don’t just add 60 styles.

👉 You must tie templates to styles tightly

🚀 Send this to your coding agent
HOOK TEMPLATE HARDENING

Problem:
Templates are too generic and reusable across scenarios.

Fix:

1. Each HookLanguageStyle must have its own dedicated template set

NOT:
one shared template pool

BUT:
style-specific template pools

---

2. Reduce generic templates

Penalize or remove templates like:
- “the way I…”
- “why did I…”
- “I really thought…”
- “I’m totally going to…”

These are fallback-only.

---

3. Add rigid templates (non-transferable)

Examples:

fragment:
- "again. really."
- "not today."

timestamp:
- "10:42pm. still here."
- "2 hours later. nothing."

absurd:
- "the fridge is winning"
- "this is a losing battle"

deadpan:
- "this was my attempt"
- "no progress"

---

4. Template scoring

Each template gets:

- reusability score (lower is better)
- novelty score
- emotional sharpness

Prefer:
low reusability + high sharpness

---

5. Hard validation

Reject hooks if:
- same structure as previous hooks
- easily swappable across 3+ scenarios
- starts with banned openers (overused patterns)

---

Goal:
Templates should force unique expression,
not allow easy reuse.

HOOK TEMPLATE SYSTEM + SCROLL-STOPPING UPGRADE

PART 1 — TEMPLATE SYSTEM (100+ TEMPLATES)

NO shared generic templates.

---

STRUCTURE:

type HookTemplate = {
  id: string
  style: HookLanguageStyle
  text: string
  rigidityScore: number   // lower = less reusable = better
  sharpnessScore: number  // higher = more emotional impact
}

- "just like that."
- "and we're done."

- "the problem is watching me"
- "i’m losing to this"
- "this thing has power over me"
- "it’s not looking good"
- "this is not going my way"
- "this is bigger than me"
- "the situation escalated"
- "i am not in control here"

---

DEADPAN / BLUNT

- "this was my attempt"
- "no progress made"
- "i did not do it"
- "nothing happened"
- "this failed"
- "i gave up"
- "this is unfinished"
- "still undone"
- "not completed"
- "this didn’t work"

---

CONFESSION

- "i said i would. i didn’t."
- "i was not serious"
- "i knew this would happen"
- "i did not try that hard"
- "i avoided it again"
- "i gave up early"
- "i lied to myself"
- "i’m not doing it today"
- "i tried. briefly."
- "i already quit"

---

OBSERVATION

- "this keeps happening"
- "it always ends like this"
- "nothing ever changes"
- "this is the pattern"
- "same result every time"
- "this is predictable"
- "this is consistent"
- "this repeats daily"
- "we know how this ends"
- "this cycle continues"

---

NARRATIVE

- "i opened it. then closed it."
- "i tried. it didn’t work."
- "i started. then stopped."
- "i got there. then left."
- "i thought about it. that’s it."
- "i went to do it. didn’t."
- "i checked. then ignored it."
- "i saw it. walked away."
- "i started strong. ended early."
- "i made it worse."

---

EMOTIONAL SPIKE

- "this ruined my mood"
- "i hate this"
- "this is stressful"
- "this is annoying"
- "this is too much"
- "this is exhausting"
- "i’m over it"
- "this is frustrating"
- "this is not okay"
- "this feels bad"

---

SELF-AWARE / META

- "this is on me"
- "i know what i’m doing"
- "this is my fault"
- "i’m aware of this"
- "i see the pattern"
- "i keep doing this"
- "this is predictable"
- "i already know"
- "this is expected"
- "i’m not surprised"

scrollStopScore (0–10)

- creates tension
- feels abrupt
- is incomplete / fragment
- implies something went wrong
- is emotionally sharp
- breaks expected sentence structure

---

+3 if fragment or abrupt
+2 if emotionally charged
+2 if unusual structure
+2 if not a full sentence
+1 if highly specific

---

PENALTIES:

-3 if generic sentence
-3 if reusable across scenarios
-2 if “safe” phrasing
-2 if over-explained

  - "why did I"

- Scroll-stopping (grabs attention)
- Compulsion (forces continuation)
- Relatability (feels like me)

UNIFIED HOOK INTENT SYSTEM

Replace separate hook upgrade layers with ONE axis:

type HookIntent =
  | "scroll_stop"
  | "compulsion"
  | "relatable"

---

- 1 scroll_stop
- 1 compulsion
- 1 relatable

- keep mix balanced
- no more than 40% of one intent

- abrupt
- fragment
- pattern interrupt
- confusing (in a good way)

Examples:
"again."
"this was a mistake"
"no progress."

---

compulsion:
- open loop
- something happens next
- tension not resolved

Examples:
"this is where it went wrong"
"i should’ve stopped here"
"this didn’t end well"

---

relatable:
- specific behavior
- internal thought
- slightly embarrassing

Examples:
"i opened it and closed it immediately"
"this still counts"
"i’ve done this before"

---

Replace 3 scores with one:

Each intent has its own scoring rules.
